candidates.why: This is an explicit COGS-related D&A line, but it appears directly as a component beneath the selected parent row 'Cost of sales', alongside other COGS components such as labor and other cost of goods. That indicates it is already embedded in the selected COGS bucket and should not be added separately.
R&D-related, not COGS-related, so not relevant to COGS_DA.
SG&A-related, not COGS-related, so not relevant to COGS_DA.
A standalone 'Depreciation' row appears only in a lower special/non-core section with no visible operating value and is not an operating COGS D&A row to add.

variables.notes: Selected COGS reference bucket is 'Cost of sales'. The explicit COR/COGS D&A row appears to be a child component of that total, so adding it separately would double count.

candidates.why: This appears directly beneath 'Research and development expenses' as a component of the selected R&D parent row. Since 'Research and development expenses' is already selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD, this R&D D&A should not be added separately.

These SG&A D&A rows appear as sub-components beneath the selected SG&A parent rows ('Sales and marketing expenses' and 'Administrative expenses'), indicating they are already embedded in those selected buckets. Adding them separately would risk double counting. Also, the label is duplicated for multiple SG&A sub-buckets, with no separate same-level summary row.
COGS-related, not SG&A-related, so not relevant to XSGA_DA.
A standalone 'Depreciation' row appears only in a lower special/non-core section with no visible operating value and is not a clear operating SG&A D&A row to add.

variables.notes: Selected XSGA reference bucket is the combined total of 'Research and development expenses', 'Sales and marketing expenses', and 'Administrative expenses'. The visible D&A rows for R&D and SGA appear to be nested components within those selected rows, not separate same-level operating expenses to add later.
